result.answer_summary: Context storage initialized. Layer-2 workers should start appending here.

payload.observation: Daily Agent v3.0 prompt Step 7 says /context/ commits go directly to main. Branch protection on main rejected the push. Either the branch-protection rule needs a path-based bypass for /context/**, or the prompt should be updated to fold context appends into the same daily PR.
payload.proposal: Update Step 7 wording from "commit DIRECTLY to main" to "commit on the daily agent branch alongside the daily content PR" until repo-side bypass is configured. Less brittle for any model running the routine.

result.answer_summary: No code change. Proposal for owner review.

payload.observation: The v3.2 closed-loop infrastructure (PR #18, merged 2026-06-19) assumes daily-operations/live-data-snapshot.json lives on main, but branch protection on main rejects the workflow's commit step on every run. Net effect: the closed loop never closes, because Step 3 of the prompt ("Fetch live-data-snapshot.json") returns 404 after every workflow_dispatch. This is the SECOND time the agent has hit this exact branch-protection wall (the first was the 2026-06-18 /context/ direct-commit attempt, see prior worker_event).
payload.proposal:
Three options the owner can pick from, in increasing setup cost:
(1) Add a branch-protection bypass rule for actor github-actions[bot] limited to path daily-operations/live-data-snapshot.json. Cleanest. Requires admin UI 1-minute change.
(2) Create a fine-grained PAT with contents:write + admin-bypass for main, store it as repo secret WORKFLOW_PUSH_TOKEN, and have the commit step do `git push https://x-access-token:${{ secrets.WORKFLOW_PUSH_TOKEN }}@github.com/...` instead of plain `git push`. Works but adds a rotation burden.
(3) Update the daily-agent prompt's Step 3 to ALSO accept reading the snapshot from a workflow-artifact fallback (the resilience patch already in flight today writes the artifact). Most resilient but requires prompt edit, which per the prompt's own rules only the owner does. Today's PR sets up the artifact path defensively so option (3) becomes possible whenever the owner is ready to update the prompt.

result.answer_summary: Proposal logged. Today's PR implements the artifact-fallback half (option 3 prerequisite) plus continue-on-error so the workflow stops being a hard-fail. The branch-protection / secrets work is owner-only.
